issues.description: Verifier read the session spec's 'suite green' end state literally and flagged that npm run test:unit still reports 2 failing tests (configEditor-foundation, notificationsSection), even while acknowledging they are described as pre-existing baseline failures.
issues.verificationMethod: Compared the session spec's end condition ('suite green') with the provided empirical results stating 'TS unit 863 passing + the 2 pre-existing Set-026 failures'.
issues.resolution_notes: Context gap, not a defect in this session's work. The two failures are the tracked pre-existing Set-026 baseline (configEditor-foundation.test.ts, notificationsSection.test.ts), failing unchanged since before Set 052 and reported with the identical caveat in every verified session since — including Sessions 1-3 of THIS set, whose 'suite green' end states closed VERIFIED (S2/S3 by the same gpt-5-4 verifier) with TS counts 788/835/849 passing '+ 2 pre-existing Set-026 failures'. In this set's established practice 'suite green' means zero regressions against that tracked baseline; this session moved the baseline 849 -> 863 passing (+14 new pinning tests, 0 regressions, tsc clean). Fixing the Set-026 files is a separate, never-scoped piece of work that no session of this set (nor Sets 052-061) treated as in-scope. Disposition: re-verify with reshaped context (the S1-S3 precedent) per the adjudication protocol. Round 2 (same verifier, reshaped context) returned VERIFIED, 0 issues.
